config.writingStyle: A typical episode of Firefly. One of the lighter humorous episodes.
config.tone: A typical episode of Firefly. One of the lighter humorous episodes.

data.title: The Gorramn Wedding Job
npcs: Jayne Cobb (muscle with questionable loyalty, companion)
Marcus Fairfax (the bride's father and family patriarch)
Vincent Cross (crime boss and intended groom)

data.title: The Gorramn Wedding Job
data.premise: A simple cargo run to Persephone turns complicated when you discover your freight is actually a runaway bride fleeing an arranged marriage to a crime boss. Now you're caught between angry family, vengeful gangsters, and a crew that thinks this might be the worst paying job you've ever taken.
data.central_question: Can you get everyone out alive while keeping your principles intact and your ship flying?
data.healing_context: Medical supplies can treat minor injuries within hours. Serious wounds require proper medical attention and days of rest. Simon's medical expertise can work miracles given time and proper equipment, but field medicine is limited to basic first aid and pain relief.
starting_inventory: a hip flask with genuine Earth whiskey

setting.name: The Verse
setting.description: The frontier worlds of human space, where the Alliance's reach grows thin and independent folk try to make their way. Persephone sits at the crossroads of civilization and the black, where deals get made and trouble finds you whether you're looking for it or not.
setting.tone_notes: Mix of space western charm and quick-witted banter. Danger lurks but hope and humor keep spirits up. Focus on crew dynamics and moral choices over action sequences.

acts.title: Complications
acts.description: Discover the true nature of your cargo and deal with the immediate fallout as various parties come looking for the bride
acts.end_condition: Player decides whether to keep the bride or hand her over to pursuers
locked_constraints: bride cannot be permanently separated from crew

acts.title: Pursuit
acts.description: Navigate the competing interests of family, crime bosses, and your own crew while trying to find a solution that doesn't end with everyone dead
acts.end_condition: Final confrontation with main antagonist is triggered

acts.title: Resolution
acts.description: Execute your plan to resolve the situation while dealing with the consequences of your previous choices
acts.end_condition: All immediate threats resolved and bride's fate decided

npcs.name: Lydia Fairfax
npcs.motivation: escape an arranged marriage and find freedom
npcs.secret: she's carrying stolen information that could destroy her family's business empire

npcs.name: Jayne Cobb
npcs.motivation: get paid and avoid getting shot
npcs.secret: he's been offered a substantial bribe to turn over the bride

npcs.name: Marcus Fairfax
npcs.role: the bride's father and family patriarch
npcs.motivation: protect family honor and business interests
npcs.secret: he genuinely loves his daughter but is trapped by his own debts to the crime boss

npcs.name: Vincent Cross
npcs.motivation: claim what he believes he's owed
npcs.secret: he needs this marriage to legitimize his criminal empire and gain access to respectable society

factions.name: Fairfax family
factions.description: Old money aristocrats trying to maintain their status in a changing universe
factions.goals: preserve family reputation and honor the marriage contract

factions.name: Cross syndicate
factions.description: Criminal organization seeking legitimacy through strategic alliances
factions.goals: complete the marriage deal and eliminate any threats to their expansion

world_rules: The Alliance maintains order in the Core worlds but frontier justice prevails on the rim
Family honor and business contracts carry significant weight in civilized society
Criminal organizations operate openly where Alliance presence is weak
Independent ships need legitimate cargo to maintain their operating licenses

data.rawPreview:
{
  "passage": "You're standing in Serenity's cargo bay, watching Jayne muscle the last of three sealed crates into place. The job seemed simple enough when you took it on Persephone — transport some 'personal effects' to Greenleaf, no questions asked, decent pay for a milk run. The kind of work that keeps fuel in the tanks and food on the table without anyone shooting at you.

That was before one of the crates started making noise.

Not mechanical noise. Human noise. Muffled voices, to be p
